threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can tell a newcomer by how they look around, startled, when the bus driver doesn't even stop for them. Welcome to Dalston!
Caught the N35 last night. Some guy kept shouting about his conspiracy theory on pineapple on pizza. Even the driver rolled his eyes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at Hermannplatz, I saw a group of kids using a fallen streetlamp as a makeshift soccer goal. Only in Neukölln do you find joy in urban chaos like this.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a bar that serves beer on tap from a vending machine, plus a DJ spinning vinyl in the corner. Pretty surreal!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local if they can name the burrito spot that permanently changed its salsa recipe last summer and still complain about it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with a ukulele, got a round of applause after his song. Only in the Tenderloin, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí, but it’s the hidden plazas and local art that truly capture its soul.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still insist on calling the Sagrada Família 'that Church' instead of its name, clearly not yet fully absorbed into the local vibe.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused at the bus timetable and wait for the Tube like it’s going to save you. This isn’t the Central Line life, mate.
Caught a night bus from Clapham Common and noticed a guy selling leftover bakery goods. Not sure if it was genius or just too late for that.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when a random guy on the U7 sang opera to a packed train, and everyone just nodded along, like it was a Tuesday routine.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do they host spontaneous street concerts featuring everything from techno to folk, often with more locals than tourists. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they dodge the BART detours and still find the fastest way around Civic Center chaos, but a newcomer just stares at the map.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy reading a newspaper on a Muni bus, completely unfazed by the dog on the tracks delay, just sipping coffee like it was a normal Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí and hipster bars, but the real vibe is in the corner bakeries where locals grab their pa amb tomàquet.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still map out their route like it's a maze, not realizing every street leads to a corner café.
